name: ClassMate PC Evaluation Notes
description: Kastner's detailed hands-on evaluation notes for the Intel Classmate PC (CMPC) — an ultra-mobile education PC — tested both in the United States and at a teacher's college in Jacmel, Haiti where teachers and K-12 students handled the device. Covers install quirks (50Hz/220v international power cable, WLAN activation gating), tutorial guide errata, operational issues (keyboard defaults, 7-inch screen real estate, standby battery), and extensive buggy behavior in the Mythware e-Learning Class v6.0 software. Concludes the hardware is a 'fine, workable device' for small hands, but Windows XP 'in silicon' carries high IT maintenance cost and the software is buggy but promising.
